assumption_overrides.external_product_use_considered_sources: EPA CompTox,RIVM ConsExpo,EU product-use dossier example
assumption_overrides.external_product_use_compatible_sources: EPA CompTox,RIVM ConsExpo,EU product-use dossier example

notes: Derived from the compact CompTox identity/use-context enrichment record.
Illustrative ConsExpo pest-control evidence mapped into the generic contract.
Mapped from ConsExpo product_group `pest_control_products` to internal categories ['pest_control', 'pesticide', 'biocide'].
ConsExpo product_subgroup: Indoor trigger spray insecticide.
Mapped ConsExpo product_subgroup to internal subtype `indoor_surface_insecticide`.
ConsExpo model_family: spray.
ConsExpo supported routes: dermal, inhalation.
Illustrative region-specific product-use evidence for a spray pesticide.

conflicts: Compatible evidence sources disagree on product_use_categories: EPA CompTox=household_cleaner,personal_care; RIVM ConsExpo=biocide,pest_control,pesticide; EU product-use dossier example=pesticide
Compatible evidence sources disagree on product_name: EU product-use dossier example=Example Surface Spray; RIVM ConsExpo=Indoor trigger spray insecticide
rationale: Selected `EU product-use dossier example` as the primary evidence source.
Primary source matches the request region or jurisdiction context.
Primary source is marked as reviewed.
Model-compatibility assessment found no obvious physics mismatch between the primary evidence source and the request.

warnings: Evidence jurisdictions ['US'] may not match request region `EU`.
Request product_category `household_cleaner` is not listed in the evidence categories ['pest_control', 'pesticide', 'biocide']; the first evidence category is suggested instead.
Request product_category `household_cleaner` is not listed in the evidence categories ['pesticide']; the first evidence category is suggested instead.

assumption_overrides.external_product_use_considered_sources: EPA CompTox,RIVM ConsExpo,EU product-use dossier example
assumption_overrides.external_product_use_compatible_sources: EPA CompTox,RIVM ConsExpo,EU product-use dossier example

assumptions.name: use_amount_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Product amount per event was supplied in milliliters and converted with density.

assumptions.name: density_g_per_ml
assumptions.rationale: Density was supplied explicitly for volume-to-mass conversion.

assumptions.name: product_mass_g_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Converted volume-based use amount into mass using density and, when needed, a bounded pressurized-aerosol interpretation factor.

assumptions.name: concentration_fraction
assumptions.rationale: Chemical fraction in the product was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: use_events_per_day
assumptions.rationale: Use frequency was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: chemical_mass_mg_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Product mass per event multiplied by concentration fraction.

assumptions.name: aerosolized_fraction

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP heuristic pest-control trigger-spray airborne-fraction bridge informed by RIVM ConsExpo spray families

assumptions.rationale: Aerosolized fraction defaulted from application_method='trigger_spray' and product_category='pesticide' and product_subtype='indoor_surface_insecticide'.

assumptions.name: room_volume_m3
assumptions.rationale: Room volume was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: air_exchange_rate_per_hour
assumptions.rationale: Air exchange rate defaulted from the shared inhalation defaults pack.

assumptions.name: exposure_duration_hours
assumptions.rationale: Exposure duration defaulted from the shared inhalation defaults pack.

assumptions.name: inhalation_rate_m3_per_hour
assumptions.rationale: Inhalation rate was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: body_weight_kg
assumptions.rationale: Body weight was supplied explicitly for dose normalization.

assumptions.name: vapor_pressure_mmhg
assumptions.rationale: Vapor pressure was supplied explicitly for bounded volatility-aware inhalation screening.

assumptions.name: molecular_weight_g_per_mol
assumptions.rationale: Molecular weight was supplied explicitly for bounded volatility-aware inhalation screening.

assumptions.name: log_kow
assumptions.rationale: logKow was supplied explicitly for chemistry-aware screening context.

assumptions.name: water_solubility_mg_per_l
assumptions.rationale: Water solubility was supplied explicitly for chemistry-aware screening context.

assumptions.name: deposition_rate_per_hour
assumptions.rationale: Deposition sink defaulted from the bounded inhalation physical-caps pack.

assumptions.name: saturation_cap_reference_temperature_c

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP volatility saturation-cap policy for room-air screening

assumptions.rationale: Volatility saturation cap uses the governed room-temperature screening policy.

assumptions.name: saturation_cap_mg_per_m3

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP volatility saturation-cap policy for room-air screening

assumptions.rationale: Thermodynamic saturation ceiling derived from vapor pressure, molecular weight, and the governed room-temperature policy.

assumptions.name: extrathoracic_swallow_fraction

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP bounded extrathoracic oral-handoff heuristics for spray inhalation screening

assumptions.rationale: Extrathoracic swallowed fraction defaulted from the bounded spray physiology handoff pack.

assumptions.name: released_mass_mg_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Released mass = chemical mass per event x aerosolized fraction.

assumptions.name: total_loss_rate_per_hour
assumptions.rationale: Total room-air loss rate = air exchange rate + deposition sink.

assumptions.name: average_air_concentration_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: Average air concentration derived from a well-mixed room model with bounded air exchange and deposition removal.

assumptions.name: uncapped_average_air_concentration_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: Uncapped room-average air concentration before any volatility saturation cap is applied.

assumptions.name: initial_air_concentration_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: Initial room concentration immediately after release in the well-mixed single-zone model.

assumptions.name: uncapped_initial_air_concentration_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: Uncapped initial room concentration before any volatility saturation cap is applied.

assumptions.name: air_concentration_at_event_end_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: End-of-window room concentration after first-order air exchange and deposition removal in the well-mixed single-zone model.

assumptions.name: room_air_decay_half_life_hours
assumptions.rationale: Room-air decay half-life implied by air exchange plus the bounded deposition sink in the well-mixed single-zone model.

assumptions.name: saturation_cap_applied
assumptions.rationale: Whether the volatility saturation cap constrained one or more modeled Tier 0 room-air concentrations.

assumptions.name: inhaled_mass_mg_per_day
assumptions.rationale: Inhaled mass = average concentration x inhalation rate x event duration x events/day.

assumptions.name: swallowed_extrathoracic_mass_mg_per_day
assumptions.rationale: Extrathoracic swallowed mass per day derived from the bounded swallowed fraction applied to the screening inhaled mass.

assumptions.name: lower_respiratory_inhaled_mass_mg_per_day
assumptions.rationale: Lower-respiratory inhaled mass per day after subtracting the bounded extrathoracic swallowed share from the screening inhaled mass.

assumptions.name: normalized_external_dose_mg_per_kg_day
assumptions.rationale: Normalized external dose = inhaled mass per day / body weight.

notes: Deterministic-first exposure engine.
All defaults are surfaced through exposureAssumptionRecord entries.

limitations.message: Spray inhalation includes a bounded extrathoracic swallowed-fraction estimate. This is not a full regional deposition model or an automatic oral handoff into a separate ingestion scenario.
Tier-0 inhalation uses a well-mixed room average and does not resolve near-field breathing-zone peaks for spray events.

quality_flags.message: Default value for 'aerosolized_fraction' comes from a heuristic screening source rather than a curated factor pack.
Default value for 'extrathoracic_swallow_fraction' comes from a heuristic screening source rather than a curated factor pack.
Spray event was evaluated with the Tier-0 well-mixed screening model; consider a near-field/far-field tier for directed or short-duration sprays.

tier_semantics.tier_rationale: Inhalation output uses a deterministic single-zone, well-mixed room model with first-order air exchange removal.
required_caveats: Interpret the reported air concentration as a room-average screening value.
Air exchange and deposition are represented as bounded first-order loss terms rather than a full airflow or aerosol-dynamics treatment.
forbidden_interpretations: Do not interpret this result as a breathing-zone peak concentration.
Do not treat directed or source-proximal spray events as near-field resolved.
Do not interpret the result as deposited dose, absorbed dose, or a final risk conclusion.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Tier A deterministic screening boundary remains in force
uncertaintyRegister.summary: The current result is governed as tier_a and tier_0; it is auditable but not probabilistic.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Use named scenario envelopes for Tier B refinement before attempting probabilistic propagation.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Bounded aerosol deposition sink constrains inhalation persistence
uncertaintyRegister.summary: Inhalation persistence is reduced with a bounded first-order deposition sink rather than a full aerosol deposition and evaporation solver.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Treat deposition-sensitive spray outputs as physically bounded screening estimates and prefer benchmarked particle families.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Coarse-spray extrathoracic swallowing is represented as a bounded handoff
uncertaintyRegister.summary: A portion of spray mass is routed to extrathoracic swallowing with a bounded regime-dependent screening fraction.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Keep inhalation and incidental-oral interpretation linked for coarse sprays and avoid treating the handoff as a deposition dosimetry model.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Resolved assumption `aerosolized_fraction` carries default uncertainty
uncertaintyRegister.summary: `aerosolized_fraction` resolved as `heuristic_default` with `screening_extrapolation` status.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Replace this default with scenario-specific evidence or include it in a Tier B envelope as an explicit variant driver.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Resolved assumption `extrathoracic_swallow_fraction` carries default uncertainty
uncertaintyRegister.summary: `extrathoracic_swallow_fraction` resolved as `heuristic_default` with `screening_extrapolation` status.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Replace this default with scenario-specific evidence or include it in a Tier B envelope as an explicit variant driver.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Limitation `extrathoracic_oral_handoff_screening` remains unquantified
uncertaintyRegister.summary: Spray inhalation includes a bounded extrathoracic swallowed-fraction estimate. This is not a full regional deposition model or an automatic oral handoff into a separate ingestion scenario.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Treat this as a model or scenario uncertainty and keep it visible in downstream interpretation.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Limitation `breathing_zone_not_modeled` remains unquantified
uncertaintyRegister.summary: Tier-0 inhalation uses a well-mixed room average and does not resolve near-field breathing-zone peaks for spray events.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Treat this as a model or scenario uncertainty and keep it visible in downstream interpretation.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Known dependencies are preserved qualitatively, not numerically
uncertaintyRegister.summary: The output carries known dependence structures, but no joint sampling or correlation model is applied in Tier A.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Use scenario packaging for Tier B and reserve probabilistic propagation for later tiers with explicit joint models.

dependencyMetadata.title: Use amount and frequency should be treated as a behavioral package
dependencyMetadata.note: Tier A/B keeps use amount and use frequency in named scenario packages instead of sampling them independently.

dependencyMetadata.title: Body size and physiology should scale together
dependencyMetadata.note: Future probabilistic tiers should use a joint physiological model rather than independent sampling.

dependencyMetadata.title: Room size, ventilation, and duration form one scenario context
dependencyMetadata.note: Microenvironment parameters should be changed as coherent room archetypes instead of independent marginals.

dependencyMetadata.title: Aerosol release depends on spray mechanism and physical form
dependencyMetadata.note: Later uncertainty tiers should condition aerosol release on the spray mechanism rather than sample it independently.

notes: Deterministic verification and benchmark regression are available for this domain.
External validation remains a documented future capability rather than an active gate.
Heuristic assumption names are emitted explicitly so evidence gaps stay attached to the scenario rather than hidden in source packs.
Probabilistic outputs stay disabled until dependencies and validation evidence mature.

interpretation_notes: Deterministic inhalation screening scenario using a well-mixed room assumption.
Air exchange acts as a first-order removal term rather than a full CFD treatment.

requiredInputs.description: Distance from the breathing zone to the active spray source.
requiredInputs.reason: Near-field concentration depends on source-to-breathing-zone geometry.

requiredInputs.description: Active spray emission duration for each event.
requiredInputs.reason: Short spray bursts can create transient peaks not captured by room averages.

requiredInputs.description: Local near-field control volume around the user.
requiredInputs.reason: A Tier 1 split requires an explicit near-field compartment size.

requiredInputs.description: Directional airflow or ventilation context near the source.
requiredInputs.reason: Local airflow governs coupling between near-field and far-field zones.

requiredInputs.description: Spray droplet or aerosol size regime used for screening.
requiredInputs.reason: Spray behavior and local persistence depend on size-driven transport.

tierUpgradeAdvisories.rationale: Spray events can produce short-duration breathing-zone peaks that are not resolved by the Tier-0 well-mixed room model.

issues.message: This bundle is an upstream external-exposure handoff only. PBPK execution, internal dose estimation, and qualification review remain downstream responsibilities.

recommended_next_steps: Preserve `bundle.supportingHandoffs` and `toxclawModuleParams` as additive exposure-side context outside the exact PBPK request payload.
Review returned internalExposureEstimate and pbpkQualificationSummary before downstream interpretation.

notes: Deterministic-first exposure engine.
All defaults are surfaced through exposureAssumptionRecord entries.

assumptions.rationale: Room volume was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: air_exchange_rate_per_hour
assumptions.rationale: Air exchange rate defaulted from the shared inhalation defaults pack.

assumptions.name: exposure_duration_hours
assumptions.rationale: Exposure duration defaulted from the shared inhalation defaults pack.

assumptions.name: inhalation_rate_m3_per_hour
assumptions.rationale: Inhalation rate was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: body_weight_kg
assumptions.rationale: Body weight was supplied explicitly for dose normalization.

assumptions.name: vapor_pressure_mmhg
assumptions.rationale: Vapor pressure was supplied explicitly for bounded volatility-aware inhalation screening.

assumptions.name: molecular_weight_g_per_mol
assumptions.rationale: Molecular weight was supplied explicitly for bounded volatility-aware inhalation screening.

assumptions.name: log_kow
assumptions.rationale: logKow was supplied explicitly for chemistry-aware screening context.

assumptions.name: water_solubility_mg_per_l
assumptions.rationale: Water solubility was supplied explicitly for chemistry-aware screening context.

assumptions.name: deposition_rate_per_hour
assumptions.rationale: Deposition sink defaulted from the bounded inhalation physical-caps pack.

assumptions.name: saturation_cap_reference_temperature_c

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP volatility saturation-cap policy for room-air screening

assumptions.rationale: Volatility saturation cap uses the governed room-temperature screening policy.

assumptions.name: saturation_cap_mg_per_m3

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP volatility saturation-cap policy for room-air screening

assumptions.rationale: Thermodynamic saturation ceiling derived from vapor pressure, molecular weight, and the governed room-temperature policy.

assumptions.name: extrathoracic_swallow_fraction

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP bounded extrathoracic oral-handoff heuristics for spray inhalation screening

assumptions.rationale: Extrathoracic swallowed fraction defaulted from the bounded spray physiology handoff pack.

assumptions.name: released_mass_mg_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Released mass = chemical mass per event x aerosolized fraction.

assumptions.name: total_loss_rate_per_hour
assumptions.rationale: Total room-air loss rate = air exchange rate + deposition sink.

assumptions.name: average_air_concentration_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: Average air concentration derived from a well-mixed room model with bounded air exchange and deposition removal.

assumptions.name: uncapped_average_air_concentration_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: Uncapped room-average air concentration before any volatility saturation cap is applied.

assumptions.name: initial_air_concentration_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: Initial room concentration immediately after release in the well-mixed single-zone model.

assumptions.name: uncapped_initial_air_concentration_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: Uncapped initial room concentration before any volatility saturation cap is applied.

assumptions.name: air_concentration_at_event_end_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: End-of-window room concentration after first-order air exchange and deposition removal in the well-mixed single-zone model.

assumptions.name: room_air_decay_half_life_hours
assumptions.rationale: Room-air decay half-life implied by air exchange plus the bounded deposition sink in the well-mixed single-zone model.

assumptions.name: saturation_cap_applied
assumptions.rationale: Whether the volatility saturation cap constrained one or more modeled Tier 0 room-air concentrations.

assumptions.name: inhaled_mass_mg_per_day
assumptions.rationale: Inhaled mass = average concentration x inhalation rate x event duration x events/day.

assumptions.name: swallowed_extrathoracic_mass_mg_per_day
assumptions.rationale: Extrathoracic swallowed mass per day derived from the bounded swallowed fraction applied to the screening inhaled mass.

assumptions.name: lower_respiratory_inhaled_mass_mg_per_day
assumptions.rationale: Lower-respiratory inhaled mass per day after subtracting the bounded extrathoracic swallowed share from the screening inhaled mass.

assumptions.name: normalized_external_dose_mg_per_kg_day
assumptions.rationale: Normalized external dose = inhaled mass per day / body weight.

notes: Deterministic-first exposure engine.
All defaults are surfaced through exposureAssumptionRecord entries.

limitations.message: Spray inhalation includes a bounded extrathoracic swallowed-fraction estimate. This is not a full regional deposition model or an automatic oral handoff into a separate ingestion scenario.
Tier-0 inhalation uses a well-mixed room average and does not resolve near-field breathing-zone peaks for spray events.

quality_flags.message: Default value for 'aerosolized_fraction' comes from a heuristic screening source rather than a curated factor pack.
Default value for 'extrathoracic_swallow_fraction' comes from a heuristic screening source rather than a curated factor pack.
Spray event was evaluated with the Tier-0 well-mixed screening model; consider a near-field/far-field tier for directed or short-duration sprays.

tier_semantics.tier_rationale: Inhalation output uses a deterministic single-zone, well-mixed room model with first-order air exchange removal.
required_caveats: Interpret the reported air concentration as a room-average screening value.
Air exchange and deposition are represented as bounded first-order loss terms rather than a full airflow or aerosol-dynamics treatment.
forbidden_interpretations: Do not interpret this result as a breathing-zone peak concentration.
Do not treat directed or source-proximal spray events as near-field resolved.
Do not interpret the result as deposited dose, absorbed dose, or a final risk conclusion.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Tier A deterministic screening boundary remains in force
uncertaintyRegister.summary: The current result is governed as tier_a and tier_0; it is auditable but not probabilistic.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Use named scenario envelopes for Tier B refinement before attempting probabilistic propagation.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Bounded aerosol deposition sink constrains inhalation persistence
uncertaintyRegister.summary: Inhalation persistence is reduced with a bounded first-order deposition sink rather than a full aerosol deposition and evaporation solver.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Treat deposition-sensitive spray outputs as physically bounded screening estimates and prefer benchmarked particle families.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Coarse-spray extrathoracic swallowing is represented as a bounded handoff
uncertaintyRegister.summary: A portion of spray mass is routed to extrathoracic swallowing with a bounded regime-dependent screening fraction.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Keep inhalation and incidental-oral interpretation linked for coarse sprays and avoid treating the handoff as a deposition dosimetry model.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Resolved assumption `aerosolized_fraction` carries default uncertainty
uncertaintyRegister.summary: `aerosolized_fraction` resolved as `heuristic_default` with `screening_extrapolation` status.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Replace this default with scenario-specific evidence or include it in a Tier B envelope as an explicit variant driver.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Resolved assumption `extrathoracic_swallow_fraction` carries default uncertainty
uncertaintyRegister.summary: `extrathoracic_swallow_fraction` resolved as `heuristic_default` with `screening_extrapolation` status.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Replace this default with scenario-specific evidence or include it in a Tier B envelope as an explicit variant driver.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Limitation `extrathoracic_oral_handoff_screening` remains unquantified
uncertaintyRegister.summary: Spray inhalation includes a bounded extrathoracic swallowed-fraction estimate. This is not a full regional deposition model or an automatic oral handoff into a separate ingestion scenario.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Treat this as a model or scenario uncertainty and keep it visible in downstream interpretation.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Limitation `breathing_zone_not_modeled` remains unquantified
uncertaintyRegister.summary: Tier-0 inhalation uses a well-mixed room average and does not resolve near-field breathing-zone peaks for spray events.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Treat this as a model or scenario uncertainty and keep it visible in downstream interpretation.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Known dependencies are preserved qualitatively, not numerically
uncertaintyRegister.summary: The output carries known dependence structures, but no joint sampling or correlation model is applied in Tier A.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Use scenario packaging for Tier B and reserve probabilistic propagation for later tiers with explicit joint models.

dependencyMetadata.title: Use amount and frequency should be treated as a behavioral package
dependencyMetadata.note: Tier A/B keeps use amount and use frequency in named scenario packages instead of sampling them independently.

dependencyMetadata.title: Body size and physiology should scale together
dependencyMetadata.note: Future probabilistic tiers should use a joint physiological model rather than independent sampling.

dependencyMetadata.title: Room size, ventilation, and duration form one scenario context
dependencyMetadata.note: Microenvironment parameters should be changed as coherent room archetypes instead of independent marginals.

dependencyMetadata.title: Aerosol release depends on spray mechanism and physical form
dependencyMetadata.note: Later uncertainty tiers should condition aerosol release on the spray mechanism rather than sample it independently.

notes: Deterministic verification and benchmark regression are available for this domain.
External validation remains a documented future capability rather than an active gate.
Heuristic assumption names are emitted explicitly so evidence gaps stay attached to the scenario rather than hidden in source packs.
Probabilistic outputs stay disabled until dependencies and validation evidence mature.

interpretation_notes: Deterministic inhalation screening scenario using a well-mixed room assumption.
Air exchange acts as a first-order removal term rather than a full CFD treatment.

requiredInputs.description: Distance from the breathing zone to the active spray source.
requiredInputs.reason: Near-field concentration depends on source-to-breathing-zone geometry.

requiredInputs.description: Active spray emission duration for each event.
requiredInputs.reason: Short spray bursts can create transient peaks not captured by room averages.

requiredInputs.description: Local near-field control volume around the user.
requiredInputs.reason: A Tier 1 split requires an explicit near-field compartment size.

requiredInputs.description: Directional airflow or ventilation context near the source.
requiredInputs.reason: Local airflow governs coupling between near-field and far-field zones.

requiredInputs.description: Spray droplet or aerosol size regime used for screening.
requiredInputs.reason: Spray behavior and local persistence depend on size-driven transport.

tierUpgradeAdvisories.rationale: Spray events can produce short-duration breathing-zone peaks that are not resolved by the Tier-0 well-mixed room model.

notes: Deterministic-first exposure engine.
All defaults are surfaced through exposureAssumptionRecord entries.

upstreamUncertaintySummary.summary: Exposure-scenario assumptions, limitations, and quality flags remain upstream and must be preserved during PBPK interpretation.

issues.message: This bundle is an upstream external-exposure handoff only. PBPK execution, internal dose estimation, and qualification review remain downstream responsibilities.

recommended_next_steps: Preserve `bundle.supportingHandoffs` and `toxclawModuleParams` as additive exposure-side context outside the exact PBPK request payload.
Review returned internalExposureEstimate and pbpkQualificationSummary before downstream interpretation.
